records.ety: [OF. respit, F. rpit, from L. respectus respect, regard, delay, in LL., the deferring of a day. See Respect.]

defs: A putting off of that which was appointed; a postponement or delay.
quotes: I crave but four day's respite.

defs: Temporary intermission of labor, or of any process or operation; interval of rest; pause; delay.
quotes: Some pause and respite only I require.

defs: Temporary suspension of the execution of a capital offender; reprieve.
The delay of appearance at court granted to a jury beyond the proper term.

records.ety: [OF. respiter, LL. respectare. See Respite, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Respited; p. pr. & vb. n. Respiting.]

defs: To give or grant a respite to.
To delay or postpone; to put off.
To keep back from execution; to reprieve.
To relieve by a pause or interval of rest.
quotes: Forty days longer we do respite you.
